insight:
Yesterday was a solid recovery day with 8,034 steps and no formal training, which gave your body a chance to adapt. Your resting heart rate of 49 bpm shows excellent cardiovascular fitness, and that low stress level of 23/100 indicates you're handling your taper well. However, only 5.4 hours of sleep is concerning this close to race day - your body needs more recovery time.

Your HRV of 107ms sits perfectly within your baseline range of 90-112ms, which means your nervous system is balanced and ready for training. Combined with that low stress score, you're cleared for today's planned session without any modifications. Your recovery markers are green across the board.

Given yesterday's short sleep, prioritize getting 7-8 hours tonight. Consider moving your bedtime earlier and creating a wind-down routine. With just 13 days until Chester, consistent quality sleep will do more for your performance than any extra training session right now.
